channel.title: Interplay on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Interplay on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Rags To Riches: A Review by Tom Decker
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to read to you a Rags to Rich’s review written by Tom Decker, the producer and designer of the game.

I was going through my archives and I realized I’ve hardly ever said anything about Rags to Riches. I think I have one video on the making of which I’ll link below, and I think I mentioned it in a couple other videos where it just as an aside — Oh, by the way, rags to rich is this and I’ll post a video or post a link at the bottom to um in the description to the rags to riches playlist, which doesn’t have many videos in it — Thought I’d fix that. Rags to Riches was the third game I made in my career and the second game I made at Interplay, and it was a business simulation which wasn’t exactly, you know, high in my list of things to do. I tried to get switched with another programmer, uh, Mark Whittley. He was doing Lord of.

item.title: The Canceling of Fallout Van Buren...And Me
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the cancellation of Fallout Van Buren and me.

I was watching a documentary — and I’ll link it below — on the development of interplace Fallout by bun, which was 2B, Fallout 3 — it was going to be their first 3D engine Fallout and what happened? And it was a great documentary and I was like Wow, someone’s collected a lot of the facts all in one place, but there was one fact missing that I expected to be missing, which was my involvement in its cancellation. Now this is a story I’ve held off on saying. It’s like what I’m more than a year into this Channel and I held it off for a few reasons.

item.title: Castles (1991) and Stonekeep (1995)
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about castles and stonekeep, two games at interplay.

One came out in ‘95 or ’ 91 and the other came out in ‘95. I have a little bit of code in the second one but I really didn’t have a whole lot to do with it. But Sebastian Ola asked about stories about castles and ston keep and at first I thought I don’t have any, but then I thought you know what I kind of do, especially about ston keep. So if you’re interested in either those games or just what interplay was like in the early and mid 90s, this is a video about stories, because, guess what turns out, I really like talking, stories, if you didn’t, if you didn’t figure that out yet. So let me start with castles. Uh, castles came out in 1991. I did not start at interplay until 1991 and I was only a contractor during that time, which meant I would just come in every couple weeks and drop off.

item.title: Interplay After Hours Game Playing
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s Tim. In previous videos I’ve talked about playing d, and gerps and LP muds. In this video I want to talk about how my game playing really took off when I started interplay. Now I thought I was a game player as a kid, you know in high school and college, but nothing prepared me for just how much game playing went on at interplay after I started. Now this is pretty much after I got hired. So in early 92 to about when Fallout kicked into high gear in 95, which also corresponds to about the time we switched buildings.

item.title: Making Rags To Riches (1993)
item.description:
Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Rags to Riches, which is the first game I made at interplay as an employee.

So the, previous game I made there bardstyle construction set. I was a contractor so I did it all at home and I would come in weekly for them to check the progress of what I was doing. But Rags to Riches I got hired as an employee. This was in very early. I think it was January of 92.

item.title: Fallout Was A B-Tier Project
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim.

I’ve gotten a lot of questions about Fallout 2 and also especially why I left Fallout 2 in mid development. I think I’m ready to talk about that, but I’ll do that tomorrow, because to understand my answer requires understanding a particular context about Fallout. The first one, the base game. Some people call it Fallout one. I just I can’t call it Fallout one. It’s Fallout and what I want to. So what I want to talk about today is Fallout was a b tier project at interplay. I don’t know why some people from interplay deny this.
